subtopics.title: Comprehension Skills Development
subtopics.teaching_note: Literal: answer is directly in passage. Inferential: read between the lines. Critical: evaluate author's viewpoint. Context clues: synonyms (or, that is), antonyms (but, however), examples (such as, for example). Practice with Nigerian authors' excerpts: Chinua Achebe, Cyprian Ekwensi, Buchi Emecheta.

subtopics.title: Format of a Formal Letter
common_misconceptions: Formal letters can use informal language
You can skip the recipient's address
subtopics.teaching_note: Format: Your address (top right) → Date → Recipient's address (top left) → Dear Sir/Madam → Title → Introduction (state purpose) → Body (explain details) → Conclusion (expectation) → Yours faithfully (if Dear Sir/Madam) or Yours sincerely (if named) → Signature → Full name. Common topics: letter to principal, local government chairman, newspaper editor.
